channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Dynamic decrosslinking enables self-healing, reprocessability, and upcycling in polyurethane networks
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 06 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-68263-6

Dynamically cross-linked thermosets often show decreased performance after numerous reprocessing cycles, limiting their long-term recyclability. Here, the authors introduce dynamic hindered urea bonds into polyurethane, enabling its thermal-decrosslinking and offering an upcycling route.
item.dc:title: Dynamic decrosslinking enables self-healing, reprocessability, and upcycling in polyurethane networks
item.dc:creator: Qingming Kong
Yu Tan
Kaiqiang Zhang
Haiyang Zhang
Tengyang Zhu
Xu Wang

item.title: Highly efficient exciton-exciton annihilation in single conjugated polymer chains
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 06 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-67422-z

This work finds that chromophores on conjugated polymer chains are very efficiently electronically coupled together. Fast exciton-exciton annihilation ensures that a single chain can only support one or two excitons after 100 ps.
item.dc:title: Highly efficient exciton-exciton annihilation in single conjugated polymer chains
item.dc:creator: Nicola J. Fairbairn
Olga Vodianova
Bernhard V. K. J. Schmidt
Gordon J. Hedley
